lesson.menu_desc: A checkpoint that reviews lessons 11 to 14: running a meeting, negotiation and the first conditional, presenting in the passive voice, and handling objections with should and could -- plus the language to talk about your own progress and goals -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 15 -- Mid-Program Check: Progress Review
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Roberto Rezende | Aula 15 -- Mid-Program Check: Progress Review

listenings.text: Hi Roberto, this is Amy. We are halfway through the program now, so this is a good moment to review your progress. In the last four lessons you learned a lot. In lesson eleven you ran a meeting and set an agenda. In lesson twelve you handled a negotiation and used the first conditional: if we order more, you will give us a discount. In lesson thirteen you presented the product in the passive voice: the engines are made in Resende, every unit is inspected. And in lesson fourteen you handled objections politely with should and could. I am really impressed. Your confidence has grown and you are much more fluent on calls. Today, think about your strengths and the things you still want to improve. Set one clear goal for the second half of the program. Talk to your teacher about your progress, and be honest about what is still challenging. This is your checkpoint. Let us see how far you have come.

This is my mid-program review. I want to be honest about my progress. Over the last four lessons, I have improved a lot. My biggest strength now is handling objections. When a client says the price is too high, I do not panic anymore. I make a polite suggestion and we reach a compromise. Running a meeting is also easier for me now. The thing I still need to improve is the passive voice. Sometimes I forget it under pressure on a real call, so that is my main goal for the next lessons. I also want to become more fluent, with fewer pauses. My teacher gave me good feedback: I should keep practicing out loud every day. I feel more confident than before. I am ready to overcome the next challenges and reach the milestone of lesson twenty.

extra_audio.text: First, look back at what you learned in the last four lessons. Then, name your biggest strength with a clear example. After that, choose one skill you still want to improve. Next, set one specific goal for the second half of the program. Finally, ask your teacher for feedback on how to reach it.
